untranslatable_reason: This signal requires multi-contract spread analysis (May vs July vs later months), inter-month price relationships, and carry cost calculations that cannot be expressed using single-instrument OHLCV indicators. The rule depends on: (1) relative mispricing across three delivery months simultaneously, (2) cost-of-carry calculations (storage, financing, insurance), and (3) redeliver profit estimation. These require external data (multiple contract prices, financing rates, carry costs) and comparison logic beyond single-bar technical indicators.
translation_notes: KAUFMAN_DRY_13 is a fundamental arbitrage strategy requiring multi-leg spread analysis. It detects when July futures trade out of normal carry relationship with May and later months, allowing profitable take-delivery/redeliver cycles. This cannot be coded as a single-instrument backtest rule without access to: (1) prices of May, July, and Sep/Nov/Dec contracts simultaneously, (2) term structure data, (3) cost-of-carry components. Recommend implementing as a custom multi-contract strategy module that monitors spread ratios and calculates profitability net of carry costs.
